notes: Inferred from 2024 'Betting plays' + Superfecta lines (Endlessly was the long-priced underneath).
analysis: Wide-open year. Closers should benefit from a fast pace up front. Sierra Leone is the freight train to take it down with Catching Freedom most likely to round out the board.
betting_plays:
Sierra Leone to Win
Catching Freedom to Place
Catching Freedom to Show

Exacta
Sierra Leone, Catching Freedom / Honor Marie, Just a Touch
$$$ Boxed Sierra Leone & Catching Freedom

Trifecta
Fierceness / Sierra Leone, Catching Freedom / Catching Freedom, Just a Touch

Superfecta
Sierra Leone / Catching Freedom, Honor Marie / Catching Freedom, Honor Marie, Endlessly, Just a Touch / Honor Marie, Just a Touch, Endlessly, Society Man

Get Rich Quick Superfecta
Catching Freedom / Honor Marie, Sierra Leone / Endlessly, Society Man / Honor Marie, Sierra Leone

horses.name: Sierra Leone
horses.note: absolute freight train closing races. Has to hit the board

horses.name: Catching Freedom
horses.note: improved every race; closing-style; LA Derby winner

horses.name: Honor Marie
horses.note: ran practically the same race as Catching Freedom in LA. Should be in exotics

horses.name: Just a Touch
horses.note: lost Blue Grass to Sierra Leone after leading; live shot if he handles distance

horses.name: Catalytic
horses.note: looked three classes below Fierceness in Florida Derby

horses.name: Mystik Dan
horses.note: may be worth a flier only if the track is wet

horses.last_race: 4th at Blue Grass to Sierra Leone
horses.writeup:
Was one of the best two year olds coming out of 2023 with a ton of speed and hype until dropping a 4th place at Blue Grass stakes
Trainer/owner have made an excuse for the poor race (didn’t give him enough days before the race to get used to the track), so maybe it truly was a fluke
Brother of last year’s Derby winner Mage
Drew #1 post which is much unwanted
Post not as bad as it used to be, not as close to the rail to start
He is a speed horse who could use the 1st post to his advantage if he can get out to an early lead and ride the rail around
Sierra Leone to his right won’t challenge him for an early lead which could help
Has regressed his past three races speed figure wise
Interesting horse here, but ultimately the first post position draw + uninspiring 2024 season = don’t play him

This horse is an absolute freight train making up ground and closing races. Once his big rear starts romping around the final turn, if he has a clean path and can keep momentum into the home stretch he can win
He seems to run his own race though and makes his move whenever he wants
Only worry is how bad the wall of traffic will be coming around the last turn
Has made his move to win wide around the last turn every time instead of having to rely on getting lucky with a rail run which is reassuring
Absolutely will hit the board, have to use in exotics at least underneath
Will probably go off as the favorite or close favorite to Fierceness.. begs the question if worth the price to win. Will take him if more than 4-1
Absolutely refused to load into the gate at Blue Grass, not a great thing, not end of the world. He’s just a beast

Two races prior caught a rail run and destroyed down the stretch. This race was beating Just Steel and everyone by a ton in the mud. The Oaklawn he had a tough trip with a lot of traffic and couldn’t get much momentum going at the end.
I don’t hate this horse, may be worth a flier but probably only if Saturday is wet
Already lost to Catching Freedom pushing down the stretch two races prior

Continuously has gotten better each race
Only horse in field who has improved by five Beyer Figure points every race
Closing style horse who refuses to have a bad race
Drew an okay post position at 4, ideally will hop out to mid pack at the first turn and then will come down to if he gets a clean trip or not
Won the Louisiana Derby after rallying from last
Important prep race to get an idea of horses since LA Derby is the longest of the prep races. Proves that he has the stamina to make it the 1 ¼ mile race in KY
Beat Honor Marie by a neck who similarly made a move around the last turn to pass the field

horses.last_race: 2nd Place to Fierceness in Florida Derby (by a million lengths)
horses.writeup:
Lightly raced horse, however looked three classes below Fierceness in the Florida Derby
Other two races raced against nobodies
Feels like 0 chance here to finish even top 10

horses.last_race: 2nd Oaklawn, lost to Muth (Baffert’s horse not allowed to race)
horses.writeup:
Nothing overly impressive about this horse. Gets to a stalking position early and stays in the stalking spot. No takeover mentality. Feels like a classic 11th place finish here
This horse has raced the most of any horse, so he racked up a lot of pieces of points to qualify for the Derby
We pretty much know what we’re gonna get: a horse will not hit the board
Hard pass

horses.last_race: 2nd Louisiana Derby to Catching Freedom. Came from way back for second
horses.writeup:
Ran practically the same race as Catching Freedom in the LA, only lost by a nose
Closing style horse
Drew a great post in #7 – should be able to comfortably start and get into mid pack
Will be a popular pick outside of the top tier of horses
Has already won twice at Churchill Downs
Should be included underneath in exotics

Very lightly raced, didn’t race as 2 year old – used to be a reason they couldn’t win the derby until Justify (his sire) won
Barely lost to Sierra Leone after leading whole race in Blue Grass
Put up a fight at the end too, didn’t necessarily die
Great post position draw, will jockey for the lead near the start
Speed figures are high for him, highest Power rating in the field
Live shot to win with his great post draw but will come down to if he can handle the added distance
People are pegging him as a speed and short race horse which, if true, would not bode well. But there is no proof he can’t extend out another 1/8th – looked decent down the stretch in Blue Grass
Will use in some exotics
Needs a slower pace to be able to continue out front at the end – but this Derby is expected to have another relatively fast pace, which would favor the closers
Encino - Scratched from Injury
Needed to win Lexington to get into Derby
Against poor field
Easily best horse out there
Three weeks ago… not enough time off
All three other races were on synthetic turf
Has improved in every race
Derby will be too big of a jump in class for him + the short rest = pass

Hasn’t raced against anything real
Barely won last rast.. gotta convince the world before I will be convinced to take a foreign horse
No
11. Forever Young (7-1)
A foreign horse has never won. Don’t waste any energy looking here. If this were the year it were to happen, there would be as much, or more, buzz about this horse similar to last year’s Derma Sotogake
There is 0 chance I pick a Japan horse until one wins, so if he beats me, he beats me

Expect this horse to try and set the pace and be out front in first half of the race
Got passed at end of LA Derby by Catching Freedom and Honor Marie, but still finished strong
Almost beat Sierra Leone in a mud-fest at the Risen Star earlier this year
Always makes his way to the front and doesn’t exactly die at the end, just has gotten passed. However those have all been perfect trips for him getting out front early and having no traffic
Any bit of traffic and this horse could fall apart

horses.last_race: 2nd at Jeff Ruby to Endlessly by a ton
horses.writeup:
No chance here. Again with 10 races of proof of work to look at, we know he will not do well
If can do head-to-head horse bets, fade this horse

Synthetic only track running in his career and/or grass. Never raced on dirt
If all his prior races were on dirt, he might be in conversation of being a favorite
However his trainer has publicly said that if he thought the horse was a dirt horse, he already would’ve been running on dirt
Trainer wanted him to enter the turf race at Churchill this weekend while the owner wouldn’t give up the opportunity to put a horse in the Derby
Closing style horse, and has looked strong in back stretch of past races
Honestly for the price I don’t hate this horse as long as he’s 35-1 or more
May play underneath in exotics to finish 3rd or 4th which could add some juice on a payout

horses.last_race: Won Tampa Bay Derby (super slow paced race)
horses.writeup:
Horse has decent speed figures
Has only been a part of slower races, so hard to know what he would do in a fast paced race, if he could handle it
Did come in 2nd at the Holy Bull where Fierceness got 3rd
Impressive stalking race and beat Fierceness down the stretch
Very alright horse, wouldn’t be surprised if he finishes 5th-8th
Not enough to make me want to actually take him with expectations
Tampa Bay is a super long layoff before the Derby (8ish weeks)
I think this is some stat that hasn’t been won from in a while

horses.last_race: 3rd at Florida Derby way behind Fierceness
horses.writeup:
Third place machine in the middest of races
Not spending any time here. Has no chance

Won Florida Derby after a perfect start and unimpeded rail run, though won by 20 lengths
Race prior – had a tough start bunched up and had to go wide to start. Couldn’t overcome and fell flat in final stretch coming up in third
Taking this race more into consideration than the free run around the track in Florida Derby since this was tougher and had adversity that will inevitably similar in the Kentucky Derby. Before the race on Saturday people will only talk about what he did at Florida in the graphics and he will get bet up too much
Every other race has either been a blowout win, or a tough trip that he didn’t win
Feels like all dependent on getting an early lead and a good final turn, which is hard to bet on. Coming out of the 17 post (famously winless in Derby history) will be tough, he’ll have to expend some juice to race across to get in good position by the first turn
Probably the best horse in the race on paper. Just is a question of worth the low payout. Feels like a pass and take chances elsewhere

Has two stalking victories which is important in the derby. But speed rating isn’t too high, and they’re usually already inflated coming out of the California circuit
Not overly impressive of a horse, don’t see him finishing near the top. Toss

Maybe an improving horse to look out for. Stalked win in Wood Memorial
Wood Memorial stunk this year, not impressive
Though I’m high on Society Man per his odds of being 100-1, beating him by two lengths there is not too inspiring
Also a horse straight up sommersalted and died during this race. crazy
Tough post position for horse with little early speed
Don’t think he’ll be worth a bet

Has improved each of past three races which is important
Was boxed in during entire last turn at wood memorial but navigated out and finished strong. Resilience just had an easier break out of the turn which helped him win
It felt like Society Man had another gear he didn’t turn loose but wanted to
Was 110-1 odds in the Wood – already a long shot to get 2nd place there
I see no difference between Resilience and Society Man, except Society Man has continued to improve each race
Drawing post 20 isnt too helpful, but if he can get into mid pack and somehow get a clean rail run, he is completely live as horse that can hit the board
If you can bet head to head between horses somewhere, look for this horse vs any other long shot and hammer
Live super long shot worth a try with a little

horses.last_race: 3rd in Blue Grass behind Sierra Leone and Just a Touch
horses.writeup:
Has improved in each race consistently, another material improvement would put him at one of the top speed figures
Had a very clean trip in the Blue Grass and still couldn’t threaten at the end. Ran out of gas. Won’t have near of a clean trip as this in the Derby
In the race because Encino scratched on Wednesday
Major Qualifying Race Recaps
Louisiana Derby (1 3/16 Mile)
Result: Catching Freedom, Honor Marie, Tuscan Gold, Track Phantom
Catching Freedom and Honor Marie both sat in last in the whole race then caught everyone on the final turn. Honor Marie lost by a nose to Catching Freedom, so I don’t see any difference between the two horses other than Honor Marie at least didn’t settle in the very back like Catching Freedom. Can’t get away with coming from very last in the Derby.
Poor quality horses, not an impressive come from behind win
Track Phantom led from the start and died at the end. Not impressed
Tuscan Gold stalked the pace the entire time then navigated some traffic on last turn impressively and finished strong passing Track Phantom
Horse(s) to use in Derby: Honor Marie underneath in a closing bid for 3rd or 4th . Considering a flier on Tuscan Gold
Arkansas Derby (1 1/8 Mile)
Result: Muth, Just Steel, then four lengths to Mystik Dan
Muth wont be in Derby because Baffert trained him and is still suspended from Churchill
Probably would be third favorite had he been running
Just Steel actually looked pretty strong here, kept pace with Muth every bit of the back stretch except for the fact that Muth was already a little forward starting around the turn
Wood Memorial (1 1/8 Mile)
Result: Resilience, Society Man, Protective
Resilience started inside post with a good start, gave up lead to avoid pressing too early. Stalked pace in second most of race and navigated decent traffic to get around on final turn and won by a couple lengths
Society Man with a tougher trip boxed in during entire final turn then seemed every bit of strong in stretch as Resilience. Swap their position on the turn and probably would have won
Blue Grass (1 1/8 Mile)
Result: Sierra Leone, Just a Touch, Epic Ride, Dornoch
Sierra Leone came from sitting in dead last w/ a very strong close out extended win over good front horses. Navigated well, wasn’t just a free runway
Just a Touch pushed the leader entire race then was caught by Sierra Leone in stretch
Dornoch stayed in 4th pretty much full race with a rail run
Horse(s) to use in Derby: Sierra Leone look for a late run at the win
Jeff Ruby (1 1/8 Mile)
Result: Endlessly, West Saratoga, Seize the Grey
Endlessly was the favorite, stalked and closed and extended lead at the end
Horrible quality of horses, this wont tell us much
Synthetic dirt track
Santa Anita (1 1/8 Mile)
Result: Stronghold, then a bunch of nerds
Patient stalking by Stronghold before picking his spot around the last turn to close down the stretch. Won by a nose
Florida Derby (1 1/8 Mile)
Results: Fierceness by 20 lengths… then Catalytic
Fierceness had a perfect start out the gate from the outside straight to the front, didn’t ever trail, turned inside each rail and blew out the field in final stretch without a challenge
Horse(s) to use in Derby: Fierceness looked unbeatable, though had a perfect run and wasn’t challenged by any horse in a poor? field.
Risen Star (1 1/8 mile)
Results: Sierra Leone, Track Phantom, Catching Freedom, Resilience, Honor Marie
Muddy track
Sierra Leone sat down in middle of the pack and went way wide to come from behind and blow past field
Track Phantom led race but couldn’t hold on at end
Catching Freedom and Resilience both had tough trips but finished well
Honor Marie started way back then finished strong
Overall take on notable horses:
Fierceness if he gets a clean trip would win, but coming from the outside he’ll have to go through a decent amount of traffic and expend too much energy at the start. Very well could win, but nowhere near worth the odds you’ll have to take with him
If we get an early fast pace, Sierra Leone is the best horse to mount a comeback around the final stretch. He’s navigated traffic in every prep race and ran his own race hitting the gas on his own terms. No reason he cant do that here
Catching Freedom is also a must play to hit the board and has a shot to win. He’s improved every race and has plenty of distance in him to make it the full 1 1/8th
Honor Marie had his best race barely losing to Catching Freedom in the prior race, running pretty much the same style. He also has a perfect post position, so he if absolutely alive to hit the board and/or win
Just a Touch is lightly raced just like his triple crown winning dad Justify before he won the Derby. He’ll try and get out to the front of the pack and stalk a moderate pace in which he could take over at the end. If he can hold up the final stretch he has a shot to hit the board
Do yourself and not let the buzz around the UAE horse Forever Young, it will be tempting, but even if he has a chance (which I don’t respect these foreign horses until one wins if ever), his odds will be beaten down by foreign bettors, so there will be no value
Endlessly is a strong horse though has only run on grass or synthetic dirt (a couple recent winners have come off the synthetic dirt prep, so its not impossible). The trainer was adamant he should run in the turf race this weekend instead, which tells you all you need to know. However that is also factored into his odds, and adding him to the bottom of exotics could payout decent
Guessing order of finish (Actual Odds as of Thursday), (Grant’s odds):
Sierra Leone (5-1) (3-1)
Catching Freedom (8-1) (5-1)
Just a Touch (13-1) (13-1)
Honor Marie (14-1) (10-1)
Fierceness (2-1) (4-1)

Endlessly (49-1) (30-1)
